109.code: for(int j = i; j <= cnt; j++)
109.reason: This loop accesses ans[j+1] when j=cnt, which is out of bounds. Also, after shifting, i is not decremented, so the next iteration will skip the new element that was shifted to position i.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.2 , reason:  [shift range should be j < cnt, not j <= cnt; also i should be decremented to recheck same position]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.25 , reason:  This loop accesses ans[j+1] when j=cnt, which is out of bounds. Also, after shifting, i is not decremented, so the next iteration will skip the new element that was shifted to position i.

116.reason: [This merging loop only checks consecutive duplicates after sorting, but fails to merge all same-exponent terms if more than two exist consecutively due to shifting without rechecking. Also, it starts at i=2, so if the first two terms have same exponent, they are merged, but if a third term also has same exponent, it won't be merged with the accumulated one because i increments past it.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This merging loop only checks consecutive duplicates after sorting, but fails to merge all same-exponent terms if more than two exist consecutively due to shifting without rechecking. Also, it starts at i=2, so if the first two terms have same exponent, they are merged, but if a third term also has same exponent, it won't be merged with the accumulated one because i increments past it.]
